issues.description: Claims left-endpoint sums underestimate / right-endpoint overestimate on increasing functions (and the reverse on decreasing), and that 'they ask it on the multiple choice every single year.' The over/under-estimate behavior is standard but not stated in the terse reference (LIM 6.2). The 'every single year' frequency claim is an unverifiable exam-frequency assertion absent from the reference.
issues.fix: Soften the frequency claim to 'frequently tested' rather than 'every single year', or omit it; the over/under-estimation rule itself is fine as standard elaboration.

issues.description: States that when the upper bound is g(x) the chain rule applies and you multiply by g'(x). This is a correct standard extension of FTC Part 1 (FUN 6.4) but is not explicitly present in the terse reference.
issues.fix: No correction needed; flagged only because the chain-rule extension is not in the provided reference text.

issues.description: Asserts FTC Part 1 is 'the most-misstated theorem on the AP exam' (09 makes a parallel 'single most-missed property' claim for bound-swap). The reference lists the bound-sign error as a common trap but does not rank any item as the single most-missed/misstated; these superlative frequency rankings are unsupported.
issues.fix: Reframe as 'a commonly misstated theorem' / 'a commonly missed property' to match the reference's 'common AP traps' framing without an unverifiable ranking.

notes: All formulas, named theorems (FTC Parts 1 and 2), the 'highest-weighted unit' claim, and both worked numeric examples (∫1→3 2x dx = 8; ∫0→2 3x²+1 dx = 10) are correct and supported; only unverifiable exam-frequency superlatives and standard-but-unstated elaborations are flagged as minor.
